meaning: n.后座议员(指英国下院的后坐议员,普通议员)
examples: One prominent new backbencher says the Big Society is already "dead in the water."
One prominent new backbencher says the Big Society is already “dead in the water.”
As a result, not being a backbencher but leaving parliament is the right thing to do.
Pro-European Tory backbencher Ian Taylor said his party had campaigned on the "wrong strategy".
Other supporters of the process include Tory backbencher Douglas Carswell and Labour former minister Frank Field.
